rag_rules: Place assumptions in component type annexes when referencing ports or variables from the AADL type scope.
Use only identifiers exposed as ports, constants, or variables in the AADL model scope—do not assume AADL property names are visible.
generic_rules: Assumptions must reference only typed entities that are explicitly declared in the AADL model's public interface.
Range comparisons must use numeric scalar types; avoid Boolean or opaque data in arithmetic or comparison expressions.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes use the syntax: annex agree {** ... **};
Boolean expressions must match the underlying data type—use eq range_value : bool = x >= min and x <= max for safe range declarations.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties like Period, Deadline, or Stack_Size as if they are AGREE variables.
Do not convert Boolean or enumeration ports into integer comparisons without explicit numeric representation in the model.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
